criteria.label: State the Multinomial NB log-score for a two-class word-count example.
criteria.example: Given counts (free=2, win=1) and log θ_spam = (−2, −3), write log P(spam|x) ∝ log P(spam) + 2·(−2) + 1·(−3) and compare to ham.

criteria.label: Build and evaluate a sklearn Multinomial NB text classifier.
criteria.example: Train Pipeline(CountVectorizer(max_features=3000), MultinomialNB(alpha=1.0)) on a labeled CSV of texts; report accuracy on a held-out split.

criteria.label: Choose Multinomial vs Bernoulli NB for a stated text task.
criteria.example: For 160-character SMS spam, justify Bernoulli NB with binary=True; for multi-paragraph reports, justify Multinomial NB with count vectors.

criteria.label: Explain why Laplace smoothing fixes zero-frequency failures.
criteria.example: Show that θ_y,word = (count + α) / (total + α·V) stays positive for α > 0, so a unseen-in-class word no longer zeroes the posterior.
